name: Core Scientific, Inc.

profile.description: Core Scientific, Inc. provides high-density colocation services and digital asset mining infrastructure in the United States. Operating through Colocation, Digital Asset Self-Mining, and Digital Asset Hosted Mining segments, the company offers space, power, cooling, and security services for AI/ML and HPC workloads, as well as Bitcoin mining operations. The company is pivoting from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure, with a $10B+ contracted revenue pipeline through its CoreWeave partnership spanning approximately 590 MW of HPC capacity across multiple data center sites. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Dover, Delaware.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Core Scientific has executed one of the most dramatic business model transformations in recent years — pivoting from a pure Bitcoin mining company that filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in December 2022 to an AI/HPC infrastructure provider with $10B+ in contracted revenue through CoreWeave [1][2]. The company emerged from bankruptcy, relisted on NASDAQ, and secured transformative partnerships that have redefined its business. This is a textbook case of a company reinventing itself through crisis. AI Deployment (2/2): Core Scientific is not merely deploying AI — it IS AI infrastructure. The company provides high-density colocation services specifically designed for AI and machine learning workloads, with 350 MW energized and over 180 MW online and billing as of Q1 2026 [1]. The CoreWeave partnership represents more than $10B in projected AI infrastructure revenue over 12 years. Market Share (1/2): Core Scientific is emerging as a meaningful player in the AI data center colocation space, with nearly $2B of infrastructure installed and plans to reach 590 MW of HPC capacity, but it faces competition from established hyperscalers and data center operators [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Clear pipeline visibility with plans to increase billable capacity from 243 MW to 450 MW by end of summer 2026 and 590 MW by early 2027 [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): As an infrastructure company with 325 employees, Core Scientific's R&D spending is not significant relative to revenue — the company builds and operates data centers rather than developing proprietary technology.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Core Scientific's purpose statement centers on providing infrastructure for digital asset mining and AI/HPC workloads. This is a generic business description interchangeable with any data center or infrastructure provider — there is no articulated deeper purpose or vision of a better future beyond building data centers for clients [2]. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable giving, community investment programs, or social impact initiatives beyond standard corporate operations. The company's 325-employee size limits its community footprint [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): CHRO Sean Kimble brings 30+ years of HR leadership experience and focuses on talent development and organizational transformation, suggesting investment in people strategy [2]. However, the company's small size (325 employees), recent bankruptcy, and rapid transformation make retention data unreliable as a signal. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Adam Sullivan maintains equity holdings, and the pending CoreWeave acquisition (all-stock transaction) aligns management interests with shareholders. However, no specific net insider buying data was identified [1].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics, with several core metrics unavailable (D/E, ROE null). Revenue growth YoY of 44.9% is exceptional, scoring a perfect 1.0, but this is the only strong metric [4]. Forward P/E of 98.3 far exceeds the 60 floor, scoring 0. Profit margin of 0% scores 0. The 3-year revenue CAGR of -20.6% (reflecting the 2022 bankruptcy and mining downturn) scores 0 [4]. Q1 2026 reported a net loss of $347.2M driven by $266.5M in non-cash impairment charges [1]. Adjusted EBITDA improved to $4.4M from -$6.1M year-over-year, showing early operational progress. The company's financial profile reflects a pre-profitability infrastructure build-out phase. Liquidity is adequate at $402M+ daily traded value.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Core Scientific IS AI infrastructure — the company provides the physical data centers, power, cooling, and colocation services that AI workloads require to operate [1]. AI cannot disrupt a company whose entire value proposition is enabling AI. The $10B+ CoreWeave contract validates that Core Scientific's physical infrastructure is essential to the AI buildout, and demand for AI data center capacity continues to exceed supply globally. The pending CoreWeave acquisition further validates the strategic value of Core Scientific's assets to the AI ecosystem [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Core Scientific generates direct, quantifiable revenue from AI infrastructure services. Q1 2026 revenue of $115.2M (up 45% YoY) is increasingly driven by AI/HPC colocation services, and the $10B+ CoreWeave pipeline over 12 years represents the most significant AI revenue commitment of any company in the digital infrastructure space [1].

infinite_game.summary: Core Scientific demonstrates remarkable existential flexibility — the pivot from Bitcoin mining through bankruptcy to AI infrastructure provider is one of the most dramatic business model transformations in recent corporate history. However, the company lacks a compelling Just Cause beyond building data centers, its young organizational age (founded 2017) and small size (325 employees) limit the evidence base for trusting teams and cultural depth, and the pending CoreWeave acquisition raises questions about the company's ability to chart its own infinite game going forward.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (0/3): Core Scientific does not articulate a Just Cause in Sinek's terms. The company exists to build and operate data center infrastructure for digital asset mining and AI workloads — this is a business description, not a vision of a future state so compelling that people sacrifice to advance it. The company's communications focus on revenue pipeline, capacity buildout, and shareholder value rather than a deeper purpose [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The appointment of a seasoned CHRO (Sean Kimble, 30+ years experience) signals investment in people strategy, and CEO Adam Sullivan's leadership through bankruptcy and transformation demonstrates the ability to rally teams through extreme adversity [2]. However, the company's very small size (325 employees), recent bankruptcy, and rapid business model pivot create significant organizational uncertainty. No evidence of exceptional trust practices or long employee tenure exists for a company this young and volatile.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of Core Scientific acknowledging competitors' strengths or framing competition as motivation for self-improvement. The company's communications focus on its own capacity buildout and revenue pipeline rather than competitive dynamics. The pending acquisition by CoreWeave effectively removes the company from independent competitive play [1].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Core Scientific has executed one of the most dramatic strategic pivots in recent corporate history — transforming from a pure Bitcoin mining company that filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in December 2022 into an AI/HPC infrastructure provider with $10B+ in contracted revenue [1][2]. The company abandoned its core business model (self-mining Bitcoin) in service of a fundamentally different value proposition (AI data center colocation), invested nearly $2B in new infrastructure, and secured transformative partnerships. This pivot was driven by opportunity rather than desperation — the company recognized that its power and cooling infrastructure was more valuable for AI than for mining.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): CEO Adam Sullivan demonstrated courage in leading Core Scientific through Chapter 11 bankruptcy, restructuring the business, and making the bold bet to pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure — a decision that required abandoning the company's founding business model and investing billions in new capabilities [1]. However, the courage was partly born of necessity (the company was bankrupt), and the CoreWeave acquisition suggests the leadership chose a buyout over building an independent long-term company.

investment_thesis: Core Scientific reported Q1 2026 revenue of $115.2M (up 45% YoY) as its pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC infrastructure gains momentum, with 350 MW energized and over 180 MW online and billing [1]. The $10B+ CoreWeave partnership spanning 590 MW of contracted HPC capacity over 12 years provides exceptional long-term revenue visibility. The company's stock has risen from its 2025 low of $10.93 to $29.16, reflecting investor recognition of the AI infrastructure opportunity. The pending CoreWeave acquisition (all-stock) would integrate Core Scientific into one of the largest AI cloud platforms, potentially providing scale advantages, capital access, and operational synergies that accelerate the buildout [2]. The company installed nearly $2B of infrastructure and supported 5 million+ labor hours, demonstrating execution capability.
thesis_continued: The bear case is significant. Core Scientific reported a net loss of $347.2M in Q1 2026 driven by $266.5M in non-cash impairment charges, and profit margin is effectively 0% [1][4]. The company emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2024 — a major governance red flag that signals prior management failure. Forward P/E of 98.3x reflects extreme valuation relative to current earnings capacity. The 3-year revenue CAGR of -20.6% reflects the volatile nature of the business through the mining downturn and strategic pivot [4]. The pending CoreWeave acquisition introduces uncertainty about the ultimate ownership structure, shareholder dilution, and strategic direction. The company's heavy dependence on a single customer (CoreWeave) for the majority of its forward revenue creates concentration risk, and the broader AI infrastructure buildout faces potential risks from energy cost inflation, regulatory constraints on data center expansion, and the cyclicality of AI capex spending.
key_catalysts: Capacity expansion from 243 MW to 450 MW by end of summer 2026 and 590 MW by early 2027, with each MW of incremental AI colocation capacity generating significantly higher revenue per MW than Bitcoin mining

CoreWeave acquisition completion would provide access to CoreWeave's customer base, capital resources, and AI cloud platform, potentially accelerating Core Scientific's buildout timeline and revenue ramp

Continued growth in global AI compute demand creating structural undersupply of data center capacity, with Core Scientific's permitted and energized sites providing a competitive advantage in speed-to-market
key_risks: Net loss of $347.2M in Q1 2026 and 0% profit margin demonstrate that the company is pre-profitability — any delay in capacity buildout or customer deployment could extend the path to breakeven and strain liquidity

Chapter 11 bankruptcy in December 2022 is a governance red flag, and the pending CoreWeave acquisition (all-stock) introduces shareholder dilution risk and strategic uncertainty about the combined entity's direction

Heavy revenue concentration with CoreWeave as the dominant customer creates single-counterparty risk — any financial distress, strategic pivot, or contract renegotiation by CoreWeave would severely impact Core Scientific's revenue outlook
